text.title: python-typing Migration Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring an untyped or partially-typed module to mypy strict on Python 3.12+.

section.title: Steps
section.p: Each step has explicit input, action, and output. Decision gates abort the migration if the floor or budget is wrong.

list.item: Input: target module path, `pyproject.toml`, Python floor.
Action: run `mypy --strict <module> 2>&1 | tee baseline.txt`. Count errors, count `Any` occurrences via `grep -RnE ': *Any|-> *Any|dict\[str, *Any\]' <module>`.
Output: `baseline.txt` plus `before` counts populated in the output contract.
Decision gate: if Python floor < 3.10, abort and route to "upgrade interpreter floor first".
Action: `ruff check --select UP,ANN,TCH --fix <module>`. This upgrades `Optional[X]` → `X | None`, `List[X]` → `list[X]`, moves type-only imports under `if TYPE_CHECKING:`, and flags annotation gaps.
Output: diff with mechanical migrations applied.

section.title: Step 3 — Stub wiring and per-module overrides

list.item: Input: list of third-party packages flagged by mypy as missing stubs.

Action: for Django projects add `django-stubs` + `djangorestframework-stubs` and wire the plugin in `[tool.mypy]`. For other deps, add `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]` with `ignore_missing_imports = true` scoped to the named packages only. Add `tests.*` and `migrations.*` overrides.
Input: module list, ordered by dependency depth (leaf modules first).

Action: for each module, add real annotations until `mypy --strict <module>` is clean. Replace `dict[str, Any]` boundaries with `TypedDict`. Replace ABC-style contracts with `Protocol`. Use PEP 695 syntax for generics on 3.12+.
Output: annotated module + 0 mypy errors on it.
Decision gate: if one module requires > 5 scoped ignores, stop and split — likely a missing model.
Input: clean repository, mypy strict passing locally.

Action: add `mypy --strict` to the CI workflow as a required check. Add `templates/no-new-any.sh` to pre-commit. Enable `warn_unused_ignores = true` so stale ignores fail when their root cause is fixed.
Output: CI red on regressions; final report with `errors.after = 0`.
